message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-o97vk (cycle/wheel adj order)

Fixed in commit ab8876f.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -65,7 +65,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mnrem","title":"parity: fnx.approximation.* exposed (G, *args, **kwargs) catch-all signatures, hiding all real kwargs","description":"_ApproximationNamespace dynamically wrapped each approximation function as (G, *args, **kwargs), so inspect.signature(fnx.approximation.X) returned a useless catch-all for every function. nx exposes the documented per-function signatures (average_clustering(G, trials, seed), christofides(G, weight, tree), greedy_tsp(G, weight, source), etc). 27 functions affected. Drop-in callers couldn't see what kwargs are accepted via introspection, IDEs and type-checkers couldn't help, and typos passed silently into nx's **kwargs handling. Fix: functools.wraps(nx_func) on the wrapper so the original signature/name/docstring are preserved. 30 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:41:51.191871760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:42:37.315185389Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:42:37.313339806Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 63a0163. _ApproximationNamespace.__getattr__ now uses functools.wraps so every fnx.approximation.X carries nx's exact signature, name, and docstring. 30 parity tests freeze the contract; all 27 previously-mismatched functions now pass introspection.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-mpv5x","title":"parity: __str__ drift on view classes and Graph (name dropped, repr-style instead of bare-data)","description":"Several __str__ implementations diverge from nx: NodeView/EdgeView/DegreeView/NodeDataView/EdgeDataView/AtlasView/AdjacencyView fall back to __repr__ (e.g. 'NodeView((0, 1, 2))') instead of nx's bare-data form ('[0, 1, 2]'). Graph.__str__ ignores the graph name attribute: fnx returns 'Graph with 0 nodes and 0 edges' even when G.graph['name'] = 'G' is set; nx returns 'Graph named \'G\' with 0 nodes and 0 edges'. Drop-in code that does print(G), print(G.nodes), or asserts on str(view) gets different output. Fix: add explicit __str__ methods that return the bare-data forms matching nx exactly; fix Graph.__str__ to include the name when set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T08:36:59.610629652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.211977409Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T08:43:54.209976079Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 73c840f. View __str__ returns bare data (list/dict) matching nx; Graph.__str__ includes name when set. 15 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ntkdn","title":"parity: random_sequence powerlaw/zipf/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence (4 fns)","description":"Four random-sequence utilities from networkx.utils.random_sequence not surfaced: powerlaw_sequence(n, exponent=2.0, seed=None), zipf_rv(alpha, xmin=1, seed=None), cumulative_distribution(distribution), is_valid_tree_degree_sequence(degree_sequence). Used by stochastic generators and degree-seq sanity checks. Add wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:04:43.303897526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.064336059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T22:19:37.062433818Z","close_reason":"Shipped in b08418c: powerlaw_sequence/zipf_rv/cumulative_distribution/is_valid_tree_degree_sequence wrappers; all 4 verified against nx (n=20 exp=2.5 seed=42 powerlaw match exactly, cumulative_distribution = [0,0.5,0.8,1.0]).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-o97vk","title":"cycle_graph / wheel_graph adj-iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.cycle_graph(n) and fnx.wheel_graph(n) (and likely sibling Rust-backed generators) produce a graph whose adj-iteration order differs from nx for the closing edge (the one that wraps back to node 0).

Repro:
  cycle_graph(4):
    fnx adj[3] = [0, 2]
    nx  adj[3] = [2, 0]
  cycle_graph(6):
    fnx adj[5] = [0, 4]
    nx  adj[5] = [4, 0]
  wheel_graph(5):
    fnx adj[4] = [0, 1, 3]
    nx  adj[4] = [0, 3, 1]

The same graph constructed manually via add_nodes_from + add_edge in the same order matches nx exactly, so the drift is in the Rust _rust_cycle_graph / _rust_wheel_graph generators which canonicalise/sort their adj insertion. The Python add_edge path preserves nx's contract.

Drop-in code that iterated adj[node] of a generator-built graph drifted (e.g. for visualising edge order, BFS/DFS-strategy tie-breaking).

Fix: route cycle_graph / wheel_graph to the Python construction path (which already matches nx) instead of _rust_*. Other generators that pass earlier probes (path, star, complete) already match.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:29:01.392150888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:29:48.956770338Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-o97vk","title":"cycle_graph / wheel_graph adj-iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.cycle_graph(n) and fnx.wheel_graph(n) (and likely sibling Rust-backed generators) produce a graph whose adj-iteration order differs from nx for the closing edge (the one that wraps back to node 0).

Repro:
  cycle_graph(4):
    fnx adj[3] = [0, 2]
    nx  adj[3] = [2, 0]
  cycle_graph(6):
    fnx adj[5] = [0, 4]
    nx  adj[5] = [4, 0]
  wheel_graph(5):
    fnx adj[4] = [0, 1, 3]
    nx  adj[4] = [0, 3, 1]

The same graph constructed manually via add_nodes_from + add_edge in the same order matches nx exactly, so the drift is in the Rust _rust_cycle_graph / _rust_wheel_graph generators which canonicalise/sort their adj insertion. The Python add_edge path preserves nx's contract.

Drop-in code that iterated adj[node] of a generator-built graph drifted (e.g. for visualising edge order, BFS/DFS-strategy tie-breaking).

Fix: route cycle_graph / wheel_graph to the Python construction path (which already matches nx) instead of _rust_*. Other generators that pass earlier probes (path, star, complete) already match.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:29:01.392150888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:32:32.922772590Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:32:32.920424293Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ohxpp","title":"parity: edge_boundary returns list (not generator), descendants_at_distance returns frozenset (not set)","description":"Two return-type drifts found in extended sweep: (1) edge_boundary(G, nbunch1, nbunch2=None) returns list while nx returns generator — same iter contract issue as br-r37-c1-682kr. (2) descendants_at_distance(G, source, distance) returns frozenset while nx returns set. Drop-in code calling .add()/.remove() on the result breaks since frozenset is immutable. Fix: convert edge_boundary to a generator (yield from); coerce descendants_at_distance result to set.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:59:57.136602866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:02:25.009360090Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:02:25.007371375Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit d9a9279. edge_boundary now returns a true generator; descendants_at_distance returns a mutable set. 11 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-oiic9","title":"parity: all_pairs_node_connectivity flow_func ignored + iteration order","description":"fnx.all_pairs_node_connectivity(G, nbunch=None, flow_func=None) accepts flow_func but ignores it (always uses default max-flow). Also outer/inner dict iteration order differs from networkx for K4: fnx outer order is 1,0,2,3 while nx is 0,1,2,3. Honor flow_func by delegating to nx when provided, and stabilize iteration order (use G.nodes() insertion order). Located in python/franken_networkx/__init__.py near the existing all_pairs_node_connectivity wrapper.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-networkx","created_at":"2026-04-25T04:58:20.898200931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T05:05:56.920452351Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T05:05:56.918586339Z","close_reason":"Completed in f14208d","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ow5fy","title":"parity: minimum_spanning_tree silently runs on DiGraph; nx raises NotImplemented","description":"fnx.minimum_spanning_tree(DiGraph) returns a Graph result silently; nx.minimum_spanning_tree raises NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for directed type'). MST is only meaningful for undirected graphs (for directed, use minimum_spanning_arborescence). Drop-in code that catches NotImplemented to fall back to arborescence breaks. Fix: surface the standard NotImplemented error before delegating to the Rust binding.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:50:56.585304287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:53:48.473340991Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:53:48.471309568Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit ba0bffd. minimum_spanning_tree and maximum_spanning_tree now raise NetworkXNotImplemented on directed input matching nx exactly. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
